01_initial_request.input_data: FDG PET/CT scans and serum inflammatory biomarker levels from 36 spondyloarthritis (SpA) patients
01_initial_request.user_intent: To evaluate the correlation between FDG uptake and disease activity assessed by serum inflammatory biomarker levels in patients with spondyloarthritis
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Statistical correlation coefficients and P values between PET indices (SUVmax, scores) and serum biomarker levels (CRP, ESR, MMP-3)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Medical records of SpA patients who underwent FDG PET/CT between December 2011 and October 2022 were available. [Gap] Need to identify eligible patients with complete serum inflammatory biomarker data. [Decision] To achieve patient cohort selection, chose to use medical records review to perform retrospective patient enrollment on SpA patients, expecting to obtain 36 eligible patients with complete data.

parameters.inclusion_criteria: patients with SpA and records of serum inflammatory biomarker levels (CRP, ESR, MMP-3) measured within 30 days of FDG PET/CT scan
parameters.exclusion_criteria: patients with liver disease, other infectious diseases, or history of other chronic inflammatory and autoimmune diseases
parameters.pSpA_patients: 26 (PsA n=6, ReA n=2, IBD-related n=3, uSpA n=15)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 36 SpA patients enrolled and classified into axSpA (n=10) and pSpA (n=26), and into untreated (n=24) and treated (n=12) groups

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] FDG tracer is required for PET imaging. [Gap] Need to produce FDG with appropriate quality for clinical imaging. [Decision] To achieve FDG tracer production, chose to use HM-18 cyclotron with automated synthesis system to perform FDG synthesis, expecting to obtain FDG for patient injection.

parameters.manufacturer: GUPID; Sumitomo Heavy Industries Ltd, Tokyo, Japan

02_agent_trajectory.observation: FDG produced using automated synthesis system with HM-18 cyclotron

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] FDG tracer is available for imaging. [Gap] Need to acquire PET/CT images from enrolled patients. [Decision] To achieve whole-body imaging, chose to use Biograph mCT 64-slice PET/CT scanner to perform FDG PET/CT scanning on patients, expecting to obtain PET/CT images for analysis.

parameters.manufacturer: Siemens Medical Solutions USA Inc., Knoxville, TN, USA
parameters.CT_mAs: 100 mAs (Quality Reference mAs using CARE Dose4D)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Whole-body PET/CT images acquired from all 36 patients with co-registered PET and CT data

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PET/CT images are acquired. [Gap] Need to visually assess FDG uptake in specific anatomical regions. [Decision] To achieve qualitative FDG uptake assessment, chose to use visual scoring by nuclear medicine physicians to perform region-based scoring on 23 body regions, expecting to obtain scored PET images with positive/negative lesion classification.

parameters.assessors: nuclear medicine physician and board-certified nuclear medicine physician independently in blinded manner
parameters.regions_assessed: 23 regions (sternoclavicular and sternocostal joints; bilateral shoulder joints including acromioclavicular joints; bilateral elbows; bilateral hands including wrists; spine including vertebral bodies, zygapophysial joints, atlantoaxial joint and costotransverse joints; bilateral sacroiliac joints; pubic symphysis; bilateral ischial tuberosities; bilateral anterior superior iliac spines; bilateral hips; bilateral greater and lesser trochanters; bilateral knees and bilateral feet)
parameters.scoring_scale: 0 = less than liver uptake; 1 = more than or equal to liver uptake; 2 = more than or equal to twice liver uptake

02_agent_trajectory.observation: All but two patients had at least one positive lesion; number of positive regions and total score enumerated for each patient

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Visual scoring is complete. [Gap] Need quantitative SUV measurements for each region. [Decision] To achieve quantitative FDG uptake measurement, chose to use ROI-based SUV calculation to perform SUVmax computation on 23 regions, expecting to obtain MaxSUVmax as representative patient value.

parameters.ROI_placement: highest FDG uptake area in each of the 23 regions

02_agent_trajectory.observation: SUVmax calculated for each ROI; MaxSUVmax obtained as representative value for patient-based assessment

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PET indices are calculated. [Gap] Need serum inflammatory biomarker levels for correlation analysis. [Decision] To achieve biomarker data collection, chose to use laboratory assays to perform CRP, ESR, and MMP-3 measurement on patient serum samples, expecting to obtain serum inflammatory biomarker levels within 30 days of PET/CT.

parameters.biomarkers: CRP (C-reactive protein), ESR (erythrocyte sedimentation rate), MMP-3 (matrix metalloproteinase 3)
parameters.measurement_window: within 30 days of FDG PET/CT scan

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Serum inflammatory biomarker levels (CRP, ESR, MMP-3) obtained for all patients

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PET indices and serum biomarker levels are available. [Gap] Need to evaluate statistical correlation between FDG uptake and disease activity. [Decision] To achieve correlation analysis, chose to use SPSS Statistics to perform Spearman's correlation coefficient analysis on PET indices and serum biomarker levels, expecting to obtain correlation coefficients and P values.

parameters.statistical_tests: Mann-Whitney U test, Chi-squared test, Fisher's exact test, Spearman's correlation coefficient

02_agent_trajectory.observation: PET indices correlated significantly with most serum inflammatory biomarker levels in untreated SpA (MaxSUVmax, number of positive regions, and total score correlated with CRP, all P < 0.001; number of positive regions P = 0.012 and total score P = 0.007 correlated with MMP-3 in untreated pSpA); no significant correlation in treated SpA or untreated axSpA

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Spearman's correlation coefficient analysis between PET indices (SUVmax, scores) and serum inflammatory biomarker levels (CRP, ESR, MMP-3)

CRP_correlation_untreated_pSpA.interpretation: MaxSUVmax, number of positive regions, and total score all correlated significantly with CRP in untreated pSpA patients

MMP3_correlation_positive_regions_untrea.interpretation: Number of positive regions correlated significantly with MMP-3 in untreated pSpA patients

MMP3_correlation_total_score_untreated_p.interpretation: Total score correlated significantly with MMP-3 in untreated pSpA patients

ESR_correlation_untreated_pSpA.interpretation: No significant correlations observed between PET indices and ESR in untreated pSpA patients

untreated_axSpA_correlation.interpretation: PET indices did not correlate with any serum inflammatory biomarker level in untreated axSpA patients

treated_SpA_correlation.interpretation: PET indices did not demonstrate correlation with serum inflammatory biomarker levels in treated SpA patients

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved: FDG uptake in untreated pSpA correlated significantly with serum inflammatory biomarker levels (CRP and MMP-3), but not in untreated axSpA or treated SpA patients. The study successfully evaluated the correlation between FDG uptake and disease activity assessed by serum inflammatory biomarker levels in SpA patients.
